== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4500005004487 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛媛県新居浜市黒島107', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛媛県新居浜市黒嶋', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:黒嶋神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11678192}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kuroshima Jingū |画像= [[ファイル:Kurosimazinzya01.jpg|250px]]
Marker pillar and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|qid=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niihama|Q386628|qid=Q386628|1=Niihama}} Kuroshima 107 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Amenominakanushi-no-Kami|Q2842779|qid=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} |社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Kuroshima Jingū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuroshima Jingū''' (くろしまじんぐう / くろしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Niihama|Q386628|qid=Q386628|1=Niihama}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|qid=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is identified with Kuroshima Shrine, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former rank was prefectural shrine. It enshrines {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Amenominakanushi-no-Kami|Q2842779|qid=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} as principal deities. Kuroshima, where this shrine stands, is now connected to land, but was formerly an island. It is said to have been called Kuroshima because the island’s trees were densely dark. === Origin === [[File:Kursimazinɡu 0ɨ.jpg|thumb|Main sanctuary]] It has long been revered for safe childbirth by the divine virtue of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|1=UNKNOWN}} and has been deeply worshipped from old times as a regional and safe-childbirth guardian deity. The exact founding is unknown, but because of traditions such as those about {{ill|lt=Princess Ōku|Q985739|qid=Q985739|1=Princess Ōku}}, it is thought the initial enshrinement is old, around the Nara period. (When Prince Naka no Ōe, later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, went to the {{ill|lt=Battle of Baekgang|Q608727|qid=Q608727|1=Battle of Baekgang}} in response to aid requests from Baekje remnants after {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|qid=Q28428|1=Paekche}} fell to the allied forces of {{ill|lt=Tang|Q9683|qid=Q9683|1=Tang dynasty}} and {{ill|lt=Silla|Q28456|qid=Q28456|1=Silla}}, his pregnant consort Ochino-iratsume accompanied him. It has long been told in this area that when labor pains started in Ōku sea of the {{ill|lt=Seto Inland Sea|Q231312|qid=Q231312|1=Seto Inland Sea}} (present {{ill|lt=Hiuchi-nada|Q11569288|qid=Q11569288|1=Hiuchi Sea}}), they stopped at Kuroshima to pray for safe childbirth, and she safely gave birth to {{ill|lt=Princess Ōta|Q603057|qid=Q603057|1=Princess Ōta}}.) In the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|qid=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was treated as an imperial-prayer site of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|qid=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court|1=Southern Court}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, as one of six prayer shrines of the Saijō domain, it received a grant of 2 koku. === Subsidiary shrines === A belief says that when visiting for prayers, one takes home one safe-childbirth stone, and after a safe delivery returns two stones in thanks; then the child will grow healthy. Kuroshima Jingū’s origin is ancient, and like Ōashi-chihime Shrine and Ta Shrine in the Kawahigashi area of Niihama, it is said to date to the Nara period. As “Kuroshima Shrine camellia grove,” it was designated a Niihama City natural monument on December 5, Shōwa 60. All three torii collapsed due to the Geiyo earthquake (March, Heisei 13). The following year (Heisei 13), one new steel torii was erected. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Kurosimazingu daimatuzinzya20220128.jpg|140px|thumb|Ōmatsu Shrine]] * Tenjin Shrine: deity Sugawara no Michizane * Inari Shrine: deity Inari-no-Kami * Matsuo Shrine: deity Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami * Iwanagahime Shrine: deity Iwanagahime-no-Kami * Sarutahiko Shrine: Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto * Wakamiya Shrine: various deities including Wakamiya, also called Okō-gami-san * Ōmatsu Shrine: guardian deity of forests, in the mountain behind the main sanctuary === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuroshima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuroshima Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4500005004487 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛媛県新居浜市黒島107', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛媛県新居浜市黒嶋', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:黒嶋神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11678192}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kuroshima Jingū |画像= [[ファイル:Kurosimazinzya01.jpg|250px]]
Marker pillar and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|qid=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niihama|Q386628|qid=Q386628|1=Niihama}} Kuroshima 107 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Amenominakanushi-no-Kami|Q2842779|qid=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} |社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Kuroshima Jingū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuroshima Jingū''' (くろしまじんぐう / くろしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Niihama|Q386628|qid=Q386628|1=Niihama}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|qid=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is identified with Kuroshima Shrine, a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former rank was prefectural shrine. It enshrines {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Amenominakanushi-no-Kami|Q2842779|qid=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} as principal deities. Kuroshima, where this shrine stands, is now connected to land, but was formerly an island. It is said to have been called Kuroshima because the island’s trees were densely dark. === Origin === [[File:Kursimazinɡu 0ɨ.jpg|thumb|Main sanctuary]] It has long been revered for safe childbirth by the divine virtue of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|1=UNKNOWN}} and has been deeply worshipped from old times as a regional and safe-childbirth guardian deity. The exact founding is unknown, but because of traditions such as those about {{ill|lt=Princess Ōku|Q985739|qid=Q985739|1=Princess Ōku}}, it is thought the initial enshrinement is old, around the Nara period. (When Prince Naka no Ōe, later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, went to the {{ill|lt=Battle of Baekgang|Q608727|qid=Q608727|1=Battle of Baekgang}} in response to aid requests from Baekje remnants after {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|qid=Q28428|1=Paekche}} fell to the allied forces of {{ill|lt=Tang|Q9683|qid=Q9683|1=Tang dynasty}} and {{ill|lt=Silla|Q28456|qid=Q28456|1=Silla}}, his pregnant consort Ochino-iratsume accompanied him. It has long been told in this area that when labor pains started in Ōku sea of the {{ill|lt=Seto Inland Sea|Q231312|qid=Q231312|1=Seto Inland Sea}} (present {{ill|lt=Hiuchi-nada|Q11569288|qid=Q11569288|1=Hiuchi Sea}}), they stopped at Kuroshima to pray for safe childbirth, and she safely gave birth to {{ill|lt=Princess Ōta|Q603057|qid=Q603057|1=Princess Ōta}}.) In the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|qid=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was treated as an imperial-prayer site of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|qid=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court|1=Southern Court}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, as one of six prayer shrines of the Saijō domain, it received a grant of 2 koku. === Subsidiary shrines === A belief says that when visiting for prayers, one takes home one safe-childbirth stone, and after a safe delivery returns two stones in thanks; then the child will grow healthy. Kuroshima Jingū’s origin is ancient, and like Ōashi-chihime Shrine and Ta Shrine in the Kawahigashi area of Niihama, it is said to date to the Nara period. As “Kuroshima Shrine camellia grove,” it was designated a Niihama City natural monument on December 5, Shōwa 60. All three torii collapsed due to the Geiyo earthquake (March, Heisei 13). The following year (Heisei 13), one new steel torii was erected. === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Kurosimazingu daimatuzinzya20220128.jpg|140px|thumb|Ōmatsu Shrine]] * Tenjin Shrine: deity Sugawara no Michizane * Inari Shrine: deity Inari-no-Kami * Matsuo Shrine: deity Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami * Iwanagahime Shrine: deity Iwanagahime-no-Kami * Sarutahiko Shrine: Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto * Wakamiya Shrine: various deities including Wakamiya, also called Okō-gami-san * Ōmatsu Shrine: guardian deity of forests, in the mountain behind the main sanctuary === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuroshima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuroshima Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]
